question: Under the pinned fair-coin Penney frame (i.i.d. fair flips; both players commit distinct words of the SAME length L over {H,T}; the first word to appear as a run of consecutive flips wins -- ties impossible for distinct equal-length words, the race ends w.p. 1, so every P(B before A) is a well-defined exact rational), what is the responder's guaranteed edge V(L) = min_A max_{B!=A} P(B before A) at L in {3, 4, 5} -- every number an exact rational computed over the FULL ordered-pair census (56 / 240 / 992 cells, 1,288 total, plus a 12-cell L=2 anchor leg reporting-only) by TWO structurally independent exact algorithms that must agree by EXACT RATIONAL EQUALITY on every cell: Arm A Conway leading numbers (correlation L(X,Y) = sum_k delta_k*2^(k-1), delta_k = 1 iff the last k chars of X equal the first k of Y; odds B over A = (L(A,A)-L(A,B)) : (L(B,B)-L(B,A))) and Arm B first-step absorption analysis on the two-word prefix automaton (<= 2L-1 states, longest-suffix transitions, exact fractions.Fraction Gaussian elimination) -- with gates (run invalid on any failure): the classic published anchors P(TH before HH) = 3/4 (L=2 leg), P(THH before HHH) = 7/8, and V(3) = 2/3 (the Gardner-table floor); complement invariance P(comp(B) before comp(A)) = P(B before A) on every cell; the antisymmetry identity P(B before A) + P(A before B) = 1 on every unordered pair with both sides computed independently, never by definition; strictly positive Conway denominators; the automaton state-count audit; sigma(A) != A asserted on every word; ZERO RNG/seeds/floats anywhere -- and does the result land (bands DISJOINT by construction, 13/25 < 3/5; REJECT stated first) REJECT ('the never-go-first maxim is an L=3 curiosity -- longer words are the cheap fairness fix'): V(5) <= 13/25, APPROVE ('the responder edge is structural -- first-order at every swept length'): V(L) >= 3/5 at ALL L in {3, 4, 5}, or NULL (anything else -- a legitimate reportable outcome: the measured decay curve {V(3), V(4), V(5)} is the citable pin, including any non-monotone surprise, and neither camp's soundbite may be cited as settled)?

source.rotation_slot: ORDER 003/004 rule-3 COMPLETELY-UNRELATED-domain rotation slot, round 4 -- recreational probability / sequential pattern-race games (Penney's game over fair-coin words), a FOURTH fleet-external domain after round 1's social choice (PROPOSAL 017 -> VERDICT 019), round 2's congestion routing (PROPOSAL 024 -> VERDICT 026), and round 3's tournament seeding (PROPOSAL 028 -> VERDICT 030); fully hermetic AND fully exact: the entire world (the word census, both algorithms, band constants, the three classic anchor rationals) is constructed in-sim from the pinned constants below, zero repo/network reads in the verdict session, zero RNG/seeds/floats

game_frame.coin: fair i.i.d., P(H) = P(T) = 1/2 per flip
game_frame.words: both players commit distinct words of the SAME length L over {H,T}; the first word to appear as a run of consecutive flips wins
game_frame.degeneracy_note: ties impossible for distinct equal-length words (two different length-L words cannot both complete on the same flip); the race ends with probability 1, so P(B before A) is a well-defined exact rational and P(B before A) + P(A before B) = 1
game_frame.arithmetic: fractions.Fraction end to end -- every probability, share, and shortfall an exact rational; NO RNG, NO seeds, NO floats anywhere; byte-identical re-run by construction, platform-independent
game_frame.seed_registry_note: this sim draws ZERO seeds -- the PROPOSAL 031 registry high-water 20260767 is left unchanged; the next seeded head continues from there

arms.arm_A: Conway leading numbers: for words X, Y the correlation L(X,Y) = sum_{k=1..L} delta_k * 2^(k-1), where delta_k = 1 iff the last k characters of X equal the first k characters of Y; the odds that B beats A are (L(A,A) - L(A,B)) : (L(B,B) - L(B,A)), so P(B before A) = (AA-AB) / ((AA-AB) + (BB-BA)) -- integer arithmetic, exact by construction
arms.arm_B: independent first-step absorption solve: build the two-word prefix automaton (transient states = the proper prefixes of A and of B including the empty string, <= 2L-1 states; the transition on a flip c sends state s to s+c if s+c is A or B (absorbing), else to the longest suffix of s+c that is a proper prefix of A or B) and solve the absorption probabilities into B by first-step analysis -- exact Gaussian elimination over fractions.Fraction; a structurally different computation from Arm A's correlation integers
arms.agreement_gate: Arm A == Arm B by EXACT RATIONAL EQUALITY on every ordered-pair cell -- all 1,288 decision cells plus the 12-cell L=2 leg (1,300 total); any inequality invalidates the run

self_checks_run_invalid_on_failure: the three classic published anchors: P(TH before HH) = 3/4 (the L=2 leg), P(THH before HHH) = 7/8, and V(3) = 2/3 (the Gardner-table floor)
complement invariance P(comp(B) before comp(A)) = P(B before A) on every cell (flip every bit of both words)
the antisymmetry identity P(B before A) + P(A before B) = 1 on every unordered pair, both sides computed independently (each ordered cell computed on its own by both arms), never by definition
strictly positive Conway leading-number differences: L(A,A) - L(A,B) > 0 AND L(B,B) - L(B,A) > 0 on every cell (hence a strictly positive odds denominator)
the automaton state-count audit: transient states <= 2L-1 with the empty string present, absorbing states exactly 2 (the two full words), every transition lands in a known state
sigma(A) != A verified on every word (true by construction: sigma(A) = A would force a2 = not-a2)
census size audit: exactly 2^L words and exactly 2^L * (2^L - 1) ordered-pair cells per L

measured_quantities.V_L: V(L) = min_A max_{B!=A} P(B before A) for L in {3, 4, 5} -- the responder's guaranteed win probability under best play by BOTH sides; plus the argmin (safest first-pick) words and each argmin's optimal response
measured_quantities.O_L: the folk-rule optimality share: the fraction of the 2^L first picks A for which sigma(A) attains max_{B!=A} P(B before A) exactly
measured_quantities.S_L: the worst shortfall max_A [max_B P(B before A) - P(sigma(A) before A)] -- the price of trusting the popularized rule where it is not optimal
measured_quantities.f_2to1_L: the share of first picks beatable at >= 2:1 (max_B P(B before A) >= 2/3) -- the popularization's 'never worse than 2:1' claim read against longer words
measured_quantities.full_matrix: the full 2^L x 2^L exact matrix per L and the trap table (max_B P per A, including the famous 7/8 row) -- the census everything above is read from

folk_rule.definition: sigma(A) = (complement of a2) + a1 a2 ... a_(L-1) -- prepend the complement of the opponent word's SECOND character to its first L-1 characters

decision_rule.bands_registered_before_any_code: in the idea file ideas/fleet/penney-game-responder-edge-decay-2026-07-13.md, verbatim; evaluated in this order; the two decision bands are DISJOINT by construction (13/25 < 3/5), so the order is stated for form, not load-bearing
decision_rule.REJECT: ('the never-go-first maxim is an L=3 curiosity -- longer words are the cheap fairness fix') iff V(5) <= 13/25 (52%); checked FIRST
decision_rule.APPROVE: ('the responder edge is structural -- first-order at every swept length') iff V(L) >= 3/5 (60%) at ALL of L in {3, 4, 5}
decision_rule.NULL: anything else (the straddle -- a legitimate reportable outcome): the finding is the measured decay curve {V(3), V(4), V(5)} itself, and neither camp's soundbite may be cited as settled

reporting_only_legs_cannot_flip: the L=2 anchor leg (4 words, 12 ordered cells: the 3/4 classic plus the fair HT-vs-TH cell)
O(L), S(L), and the per-word folk-rule table
the trap table (max_B P per A, including the famous 7/8 row) and both distributions' quartiles per L (the trap distribution max_B P per A, and the folk-rule distribution P(sigma(A) before A) per A)
whether the safest first pick and the biggest trap are complements/reverses of each other (pattern-structure context, prose only)
intake_time_decisions_disclosed: word encoding: strings over the alphabet ('H', 'T'); the census enumerates itertools.product in the fixed alphabet order, so all output orderings are explicit and deterministic
matrix orientation: matrix[A][B] = P(B appears before A) -- the RESPONDER B's win probability against first pick A; diagonal cells (A = A) do not exist (distinct words only)

quartile convention (reporting-only): Q1/median/Q3 of the sorted per-A values by the exact linear-interpolation method (position p = (n-1)*q; value = v[floor(p)] + frac(p)*(v[floor(p)+1]-v[floor(p)]), all in Fraction) -- exact rationals, no floats; the same convention as verdict-030

'both distributions' in the reporting leg is read as: (i) the trap distribution {max_B P(B before A) : A} and (ii) the folk-rule distribution {P(sigma(A) before A) : A}, quartiled per L over the 2^L first picks
argmin/argmax tie convention: ALL words attaining the exact min/max are listed, sorted lexicographically (H < T); the lexicographically-smallest is the named representative in summary lines
the folk-rule legs O(L)/S(L)/per-word table are scored at L in {3, 4, 5} only -- the registered scope; the L=2 leg carries only its census, the 3/4 anchor, and the fair HT-vs-TH cell

antisymmetry-independence implementation: every ordered cell (A, B) is computed standalone by BOTH arms (no cell's value is ever derived as 1 minus another cell); the antisymmetry check then sums the two independently produced values

the Conway positivity check asserts BOTH differences L(A,A)-L(A,B) > 0 and L(B,B)-L(B,A) > 0 (each is a strictly positive integer for distinct equal-length words), which implies the odds denominator is strictly positive
decimal approximations in the report and stdout are display-only (computed from the exact Fraction, correctly rounded to 6 places); every decision comparison is an exact Fraction comparison

runtime python is recorded in the REPORT but NOT asserted in-sim: the sim is exact-rational and platform-independent by construction (no floats, no dict-order dependence -- all output orderings explicitly sorted)

model_basis.declared_assumption: a FAIR i.i.d. coin (p = 1/2 per flip) and both players restricted to words of the SAME length L -- the game's own canonical frame, the one every popularization uses; the measured curve is a property of THIS frame
model_basis.single_most_likely_to_flip_alternative: a BIASED coin (p != 1/2), under which the whole table deforms -- the Conway leading-number arm is fair-coin-specific while the automaton arm generalizes to any p, so 'V(L) under bias' is the named follow-up head with one arm already built; a softer flip is the RESPONSE SPACE -- allowing the responder a SHORTER or LONGER word changes the game (unequal-length races are out of this verdict's registered scope by design, named)
model_basis.boundary: L <= 5 only: L=6 doubles the census to 4,032 pairs -- a feasible follow-up, not a blocker; the swept range is where the folk tables live
